expanded_queries: What are the methods for establishing higher time frame bias?

final_chunks.section_title: FAIR VALUE GAPS AS POINT OF ORIGIN AND BIAS DETERMINATION
final_chunks.text:
FAIR VALUE GAPS AS POINT OF ORIGIN AND BIAS DETERMINATION
We've spent a great deal of time on fair value gaps in recent weeks and months. Much like order blocks, once you understand the central tenets, fair value gaps are fairly easy to spot in price and are not ambiguous; they are very clear.

final_chunks.section_title: IMPLEMENTING BULLISH DAILY BIAS
final_chunks.text:
IMPLEMENTING BULLISH DAILY BIAS
When implementing the daily bias on a daily chart, for a bullish scenario, we are waiting for a swing high to form and to be broken. A swing high is defined as a candle that has a lower high to the left of it and then a lower high to the right of it.
final_chunks.source: ICT - Mastering High Probability Scalping Vol. 2 of 3_Handbook.blocks.json

final_chunks.section_title: EMPLOYING BEARISH DAILY BIAS
final_chunks.text:
EMPLOYING BEARISH DAILY BIAS
When employing the daily bias for a bearish scenario, we are waiting again on the daily chart for a swing low to be broken. A swing low is defined as a daily candle that has a higher low to the left of it and a higher low to the right of it.
final_chunks.source: ICT - Mastering High Probability Scalping Vol. 2 of 3_Handbook.blocks.json

final_chunks.section_title: BEARISH DAILY BIAS
final_chunks.text:
BEARISH DAILY BIAS
You want to see the market trade up until it creates a daily swing high. When that third candle forms at its close, the very next trading day you will be watching for price to make an attempt to trade through this candle's low.
final_chunks.source: ICT - Mastering High Probability Scalping Vol. 2 of 3_Handbook.blocks.json

final_chunks.section_title: OPPORTUNITY DISCOVERY
final_chunks.text:
OPPORTUNITY DISCOVERY

final_chunks.section_title: IMPORTANCE OF DAILY CHART BIAS AND KNOWING WHEN TO DO NOTHING
